q: What was the Roman name for the goddess Athena?
exp: Minerva was the Roman goddess of wisdom and strategy.

q: What was the Roman name for Ares?
exp: Mars was the Roman god of war and gave his name to the red planet.

q: What was the Roman name for Hermes?
exp: Mercury was the swift messenger of the Roman gods.

q: Who was Zeus's wife, queen of the gods?
Which Greek hero had a single weak spot on his heel?
exp: The phrase 'Achilles' heel' comes from this myth.

q: Which young man flew too close to the Sun on wax wings and fell?
exp: The wax on Icarus's wings melted in the Sun's heat.

q: Which half-man, half-bull creature lived in the Labyrinth of Crete?
exp: Theseus slew the Minotaur and escaped the maze using a thread.

q: What was the three-headed dog that guarded the Greek underworld?
exp: Cerberus kept the dead from leaving the realm of Hades.

q: Which winged horse sprang from the blood of Medusa?
exp: Pegasus was a white horse with wings.

q: What is the prophesied end of the world in Norse mythology?
exp: At Ragnarök many gods die and the world is reborn.

q: What is the great tree that links the nine worlds in Norse myth?
What is the rainbow bridge connecting Asgard to the world of men?
exp: The god Heimdall guarded the Bifröst bridge.

q: Which Titan gave fire to humans and was punished by Zeus?
exp: Prometheus was chained and an eagle ate his liver each day.

q: Which Titan was condemned to hold up the sky?
exp: Atlas holds up the vault of the heavens forever.

q: Which Roman goddess of the hunt corresponds to Artemis?
exp: Diana was the Roman goddess of the hunt and the Moon.
